---
title: "Editorial Note"
aliases: ["/editorial.html"]
---

## The text

The German text is the Marxists Internet Archive transcription of the Dietz
Verlag (Berlin) 1965 edition of *Das Erfurter Programm in seinem
grundsätzlichen Theil erläutert* (first published 1892). Kautsky died in 1938;
the German text is in the public domain.

## Why a new translation

<!-- TODO: expand — Bohn 1910 abridgement, terminology drift -->

## Translation principles

- **Complete and unabridged.** No clause of the German is merged, summarized,
  or omitted.
- **Consistent terminology.** Core theoretical terms are fixed by a
  [termbase](https://github.com/) <!-- TODO: link to glossary.yaml in repo -->:
  *Arbeitskraft* is always "labour-power", *Mehrwert* always "surplus value",
  and so on.
- British spelling conventions throughout.

## Method

The draft translation was produced with an AI-assisted pipeline (Claude,
Anthropic) constrained by the termbase and validated by mechanical checks for
omissions, markup fidelity, and terminological consistency, followed by human
review of every block. Blocks are marked in the source data as
`translated` (machine draft), `flagged` (failed a mechanical check), or
`verified` (human-reviewed).

## License

<!-- TODO: choose and state a license for the translation, e.g. CC BY-SA 4.0 -->
